title: FinTrack — Finance App Redesign
subtitle: Making personal finance feel less like homework
overview: FinTrack is a personal finance app used by 50,000+ users. Despite good functionality, users were churning at the dashboard stage. I was brought in to redesign the core tracking experience.
problem: Users couldn't track spending habits effectively due to confusing navigation, overwhelming data presentation, and a lack of clear financial goals feature.
research: Conducted 8 user interviews across different income brackets
Ran a competitive analysis of 5 rival apps (Mint, YNAB, Wallet, Money Manager, Spendee)
Created empathy maps revealing users felt 'judged' by the app
Identified 3 primary user archetypes: Casual Tracker, Budget Planner, and Debt Manager
solution: Redesigned the information architecture, introduced a goals-first dashboard, created a spending calendar view, and replaced complex charts with plain-language summaries.
outcome: 42% improvement in task completion rate during usability testing. Client reported 28% reduction in churn post-launch.

title: Recipe Discovery App
subtitle: Cooking should feel like creativity, not a chore
overview: A self-initiated case study exploring how people discover, save, and follow recipes. Existing apps are cluttered with ads and require too many steps to get to the actual recipe.
problem: Popular recipe apps overwhelm users with ads, autoplay videos, and life stories before showing the recipe. Mobile experience is painful, especially with messy hands.
research: Surveyed 24 home cooks via Google Forms
Analysed pain points from App Store reviews of top 3 recipe apps
Created 2 user personas: Weeknight Cook and Weekend Baker
Mapped the current journey to identify 7 friction points
solution: Minimal card-based interface with instant recipe access, smart ingredient swaps, a cook mode that keeps the screen awake, and voice navigation.
outcome: Prototype tested with 8 participants. All rated clarity 5/5. Average time-to-recipe reduced from 47 seconds to 11 seconds.

title: EduPlatform Student Dashboard
subtitle: Students shouldn't feel lost in their own learning
overview: An ed-tech startup's student dashboard had a 62% drop-off rate after onboarding. Students reported feeling confused about where to start and couldn't track their progress.
problem: The dashboard showed all courses, all progress, and all notifications in a flat list — no hierarchy, no priority, no sense of direction.
research: Mapped the onboarding to course completion journey
Identified 'what do I do next' as the #1 user question
solution: Redesigned with a priority-based Today view, a visual progress tracker per course, grouped notifications, and a simplified onboarding checklist.
outcome: Client reported 30% reduction in support tickets. Onboarding completion rate increased from 41% to 74%.
